mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust household purchasing power without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent annually while CPIAUCSL remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate revenue expectations, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle recovery periods over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal generates negative 20-day forward returns in over 55 percent of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand growth that directly translates into higher corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside contracting industrial production signal a synchronized downturn in construction and manufacturing sectors, reducing capital expenditure expectations and driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive when both HOUST and INDPRO decline simultaneously during housing correction periods.
